MODULE 7: VISION
EXERCISE WORKBOOK
LESSON ONE
1. Write out your vision for the future. Ask yourself what you would like to achieve,
and what you want your life to look like in five years. Describe this vision in as
much detail as you can.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 16 --

2. Do you think your vision will inspire other people?
a. If yes, what about it do you think they will find inspiring?
b. If no, why not? How can you change your vision to make it more inspiring?
3. Name someone who has a vision that you think is worth following.
4. What aspects of this person’s vision can you apply to your own vision?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 2 of 16 --

LESSON TWO
1. List three ways you can build logical certainty for your product in your prospect’s
mind.
2. List three ways you can build emotional certainty for your product in your
prospect’s mind.
3. List five things you can do every day to keep yourself motivated.
4. List all of the things that cause you to have doubts about succeeding.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 3 of 16 --

a. Write out a course of action for overcoming these doubts.
5. List all of the things that give you confidence that you will succeed.
a. Write out ways you can build upon these attributes to achieve your vision.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 16 --

LESSON THREE
1. Describe a time when you set a short-term goal and hit it. How did you react?
How did it make you feel?
2. Describe a time when you set a short-term goal and missed it. How did you
react? How did it make you feel?
3. Set three short-term goals for yourself right now. Assign dates to each one and
track your progress.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 5 of 16 --

LESSON FOUR
1. Referring back to the vision statement you wrote out in Lesson One, write out
your “Why.” Why do you really want your life to look like that? What are the core
reasons?
a. Do you think that if you achieve this vision, it will make you happy? If so,
why?
b. Do you think that if you achieve this vision, it will make the people around
you happy? If so, why?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 6 of 16 --

2. How long have you given yourself to achieve this vision?
3. Write out the first three goals you hope to reach on the road to achieving your
vision.
LESSON FIVE
1. Thoroughly review your vision statement and your “why” from the previous
exercises. Examine them closely and intently.
a. Does your vision statement truly reflect what you wish to achieve? Does it
paint a highly accurate picture of what you want your life to look like?
b. If no, what do you need to change?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 7 of 16 --

2. Is your “why” so important to you that you would “run through a wall of fire,” to make it
happen?
a. If yes, explain why.
b. If no, explain why not. How can you dig deeper to make it mean more to you?
3. List all of the fears you have that may hinder you achieving your vision.
a. Write out how you can use these fears to propel yourself toward success.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 16 --

LESSON SIX
1. Explain how you will communicate your vision to others.
a. Being specific, explain what it is about your vision that you think will inspire
people?
2. Describe what kind of people you will need to have on your team in order for you to achieve
your vision.
a. Explain why you think each of them will gravitate toward you.
b. What do you think each of them will get out of joining you?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 16 --

3. Map out what you think your perfect team would look like.
a. What role will each person play?
b. How will you all work together to succeed?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 16 --

LESSON SEVEN
1. List all of the dream-stompers in your life. (Hopefully, it’s a short list.)
a. What kind of things do they say to you?
b. How do they make you feel?
2. Write out a course of action for either removing or silencing them as explained in
the lesson.
3. In thirty days, revisit this list and report your progress.
a. If there are still any vocal dream-stompers left in your life, start the
process of removal / silencing over until they have all been vanquished or
silenced.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 11 of 16 --

4. List five empowering statements that you would like to hear the dream-stompers
say about you that would encourage you, rather than discourage you. Keep this
list close by and recite it to yourself every morning,
LESSON EIGHT
1. Explain the difference between a goal and a vision.
2. Are the current goals you have set for yourself guiding you toward your vision or
away from it?
a. If they are guiding you toward your vision, explain how you know. What kind of
indicators are they giving you?
b. If they are guiding you away from your vision, explain how you know. What kind
of indicators are they giving you?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 12 of 16 --

3. If your goals are currently leading you toward your vision, what can you do to ensure
you stay on track?
4. If your goals are currently leading you away from your vision, what adjustments can
you make to get back on track?
LESSON NINE
1. List the main skill you are required to master in order to succeed in your industry.
2. Have you mastered this skill?
a. If not, write out your plan to accomplish this.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 13 of 16 --

3. List three people in your industry whom you feel have mastered this skill at the
highest level.
a. If anything, what are they doing that you aren’t doing?
b. What can you learn from them that will help you get to their level?
c. What action do you need to take to get yourself to their level, and beyond?
4. Take another look at your goals.
a. Do they all have dates attached to them? If not, assign each one a date.
b. Label each goal long-term or short-term.
5. Using the eight steps for setting a goal outlined in this lesson, apply these steps
to each of your individual goals and write out each of the steps accordingly as
follows:
a. What is your goal?
b. How much time did you give yourself to achieve it?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 14 of 16 --

c. List the people, groups, and organizations with whom you will need to align
yourself in order to achieve this goal.
d. What do you need to know in order to achieve this goal?
e. What is your plan of attack?
f. What massive action did you take?
g. What were your results?
h. What action helped lead you toward your goal?
i. What action took you away from it?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 15 of 16 --

j. What changes, if any, did you make (incrementally and one at a time) to your
approach to get you back on track toward achieving your goal?
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 16 of 16 --
